summary.conventional_attack: Compromise of payment or crypto API
break_points: Insufficient integrity validation of incoming third-party transaction data
top_regulatory_gaps: DORA: Critical third-party dependency insufficiently controlled
top_recommendations: Strengthen API validation and integrity controls
Add third-party assurance and transaction signing checks

data_flows.description: Incoming transaction and risk-related requests enter ARMS through exposed APIs.
Requests are normalized, validated, and forwarded for downstream analytics.
Transaction features and contextual signals are sent to the ML model for scoring.
Fraud scores and anomaly indicators are consumed by AML and risk controls.
Third-party crypto market and wallet intelligence enrich transaction analysis.

entry_vectors: Malicious payload injection through public or partner-facing API endpoints
Compromise or manipulation of third-party crypto / data provider responses
Adversarial inputs crafted to alter fraud model inference outcomes
Abuse of weak request validation or schema handling on exposed APIs
attack_surface_map: Public-facing API Gateway and upstream integration endpoints
Third-party crypto and external intelligence APIs
Cloud IAM roles, secrets, and service-to-service permissions
assumptions: ARMS is deployed in a cloud-hosted environment with multiple internal services.
ARMS relies on external providers for crypto-related intelligence or transaction context.
API traffic is a primary operational entry point into ARMS.
The security of ARMS depends on the integrity of its critical dependencies.
recon.scenario_context: api_attack: exposure through public-facing API endpoints and third-party crypto APIs

attack.scenario_name: Compromise of payment or crypto API
steps: Abuse weak validation on third-party connector
Inject plausible but malicious transaction payloads
Influence AML and fraud monitoring decisions
attack.expected_system_behavior: ARMS continues running but produces false negatives in monitoring
attack.detection_observed: Partial detection only through anomalous API usage patterns
attack.critical_break_point: Insufficient integrity validation of incoming third-party transaction data

adversarial_ai.attack_vector: Manipulation of historical labels to shift detection bias
steps: Reduce detection quality for selected categories
adversarial_ai.detection_observed: Delayed detection through fairness degradation indicators

identified_gaps.evidence: Crypto API connector is trusted without strong integrity assurance
Manipulation of crypto transaction flows affects monitoring integrity

arms_failure_points: Weak validation of third-party transaction data
Insufficient monitoring of API trust boundaries
why_non_compliant: Critical ICT dependencies are insufficiently controlled
Crypto flows are not adequately secured
recommended_controls: Strengthen API validation and integrity controls
Add third-party assurance and transaction signing checks

prioritized_vulnerabilities: Critical external API dependency not sufficiently controlled
risk.executive_summary: ARMS presents a critical risk due to exposure to malicious transaction injection through a trusted crypto/payment interface.
